name: Hot Clam Dip
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain the clams and reserve 1 tablespoon of the liquid.
2. Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat.
3. Add onion to the skillet and cook until tender but not browned.
4. Stir in the reserved clam liquid, catsup, hot pepper sauce, cheese, olives, and Worcestershire sauce.
5. Cook until the cheese is melted. Serve immediately with tortilla chips.
6. Alternatively, hollow out a French bread loaf using an 8-inch round baking dish.
7. Pour the clam mixture into the cavity of the hollowed bread.
8. Top with the cut piece of bread and wrap in foil.
9. Bake at 375°F for 15 to 18 minutes until the cheese melts and the bread is hot.
This recipe yields about 4 servings, each containing approximately:
220 calories; 388 mg sodium; 64 mg cholesterol; 16 grams fat; 4 grams carbohydrates; 15 grams protein; 0.07 gram fiber.
